title: passerina incurva
intro: Passerina incurva is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as a type of vine or shrub. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Passerina incurva include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Passerina incurva typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or poultices for medicinal purposes.
